item.title: Aviation, tourism, agriculture sectors hit by ME war
item.description: The war in the Middle East is impacting numerous economic sectors and not only in the region, both by direct disruptions and rising fuel prices......

item.title: ECB won’t be ‘paralysed’ in face of energy shock: Lagarde
item.description: The European Central Bank has several options for dealing with the Middle East war energy shock, its chief Christine Lagarde said Wednesday, vowing policymakers would not be ‘paralysed by hesitation’......

item.title: OpenAI kills Sora video app
item.description: OpenAI said Tuesday that it would shut down its artificial intelligence video generation app Sora barely six months after its launch, as the company shifts toward business tools ahead of a potential stock market debut......

item.title: Islami Bank holds board meeting
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC on Wednesday held a meeting of its board of directors at Islami Bank Tower in the capital, said a press release......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Dhaka stocks rebound
item.description: The Dhaka Stock Exchange bounced back on Wednesday after declining in the previous session following the Eid holidays, while the Chittagong Stock Exchange failed to recover, ending in the red despite most stocks posting gains......

item.title: Challenges, solutions for struggling capital
item.description: DHAKA, with more than 400 years of recorded urban history, now stands at a critical turning point. Once a charming Mughal capital, it has become one of the world’s most congested, polluted and densely populated megacities. Rapid and uneven urbanization, overdependence on a single city for national services and decades of weak urban governance have pushed......

item.title: Truth, media and war on Iran
item.description: US DEFENCE secretary Pete Hegseth appears to have little patience for questions that do not conform to his preferred style of declaring unsubstantiated victories, whether against South Americans or in the Middle East......Maps

item.title: Making sense, nonsense of ‘decline hypothesis’
item.description: THE post-1/11 Awami League government undertook several educational reforms during its valid and invalid tenures. In its earlier phases, at the primary and secondary levels, it declared that the days of ‘rote’ learning were over, and that it would be replaced by an era of what they called ‘acquisition, learning and retrieval.’ It boasted of its achievements in the education......

item.title: Curriculum reform and relevance
item.description: EDUCATION is the bedrock upon which a nation builds its social fabric, economic vitality and cultural identity. In an age of rapid technological evolution, globalisation and increasingly fluid labour markets, the relevance of a national curriculum cannot be overstated. In Bangladesh, despite steady efforts towards curricular reform, the current system still does not......

item.title: Unshackling the presidency
item.description: THE president of the People’s Republic of Bangladesh, irrespective of party affiliation, or even a non-partisan one, remains prisoner of a constitutional provision that does not allow him/her to function under the dictates of his conscience, for he is bound to ‘act’, as Article 48(C) clearly states, only ‘in accordance with the advice of the Prime Minister,’ except for appointing the prime minister and the chief justice......

item.title: Investigative delays undermine justice
item.description: THE observations that the High Court has made in its full verdict, released on March 14, on granting bail to former chief justice ABM Khairul Haque are pertinent. The court, in its short verdict on March 8, granted bail to the former chief justice, but he has not been released as the government’s appeal against the bail is pending. A police application to show him arrested......

item.title: BNP, others must rein in right wing rise
item.description: BANGLADESH observes its 54th independence anniversary in a real ironical political circumstance: the Awami League, which presided over the country’s war of national independence in 1971, now, with its entire leadership, fleeing the country in the face of a democratically oriented student-mass uprising against the League’s brutal authoritarian governance......

item.title: Rethinking Bangladesh’s military command
item.description: BANGLADESH was not born gently in 1971. Nations rarely are. Ours emerged amid the smoke of burned villages, the dull thunder of artillery and the nervous crackle of radios carrying rebellion across a frightened countryside. Independence did not arrive as a constitutional gift; it was wrested from the wreckage of a collapsing state by men and women who decided......

item.title: Nation is free, but what about its citizens?
item.description: BANGLADESH is celebrating its 55th anniversary of independence as a nation, and it’s time to ask: Are the people of Bangladesh free and independent? There is no doubt that the people of Bangladesh are free as a nation. This identity stems from the international fact that Bangladesh has been an independent nation-state recognised by the world since 1971.......

item.title: What Bangladesh is taking shape today?
item.description: ‘TELL me something,’ a friend asked over tea in Winnipeg, Canada, recently, stirring his cup like it contained the secrets of the republic, ‘are we still the same country that was born in 1971?’......

item.title: Future of democratic trust
item.description: AS BANGLADESH marks the 55th anniversary of its independence, the country once again finds itself reflecting on the future of its democracy. Political transitions, debates about governance, and calls for institutional reform have become central to public discussion. Yet amid these conversations, one issue remains surprisingly underexplored: conflict of interest......

item.title: Strategic equilibrium
item.description: SOUTH Asia is entering another phase of geopolitical recalibration. Domestic political changes, shifting economic networks and evolving security dynamics are reshaping the strategic landscape of the region. Few bilateral relationships illustrate these transformations more clearly than that between Bangladesh and India. Bound by geography yet often divided by......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: What constitutes independence for women?
item.description: FOLLOWING the July revolution, nothing remains the same in Bangladesh......

item.title: Rejoicing or reflecting?
item.description: 26 MARCH — Bangladesh’s National Independence Day — feels especially significant in 2026. After the dangers of protracted autocracy, we have returned to the promises of democracy. Yet the day is also a time for deep, sober, and clear-eyed reflection by the government and people from all walks of life......

item.title: When the river swallows its own banks
item.description: THERE is a particular grief that has no name in English but lives in the bones of every Bangladeshi who has watched someone they carried on their shoulders — whose photograph hung in their tea shop, whose name their children chanted — turn around and look at them as though they are a problem to be managed. This is not the grief of defeat. Defeat you can live......

item.title: Immodest reflections on independence
item.description: MORE than five decades have passed since Bangladesh emerged as a distinct, independent state. And yet the soil of Bangladesh remains drenched in blood — flagrantly, relentlessly, and with a terrifying continuity that mocks the very promise of independence. It is the blood of workers and peasants, of women and children, of ethnic and religious minorities — of those......

item.title: Jamaat for all-party dialogue to face ME conflict fallout
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Wednesday demanded holding an all-party dialogue to face the crisis created in Bangladesh after the beginning of attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Cultural orgs observe Genocide Day
item.description: Cultural organisations and institutions observed Genocide Day with series of programmes across the capital on Wednesday, commemorating the massacre the Pakistani military perpetrated on March 25, 1971 in the then East Pakistan......

item.title: Workers Party demands trial of Pakistan army for genocide
item.description: Leaders of the Workers Party of Bangladesh on Wednesday at a discussion demanded trial of the solders of the then East Pakistan army responsible for the mass killing perpetrated in Dhaka on March 25 in 1971......

item.title: Hiroshima Univ delegation calls on DU VC
item.description: A four-member delegation led by Professor Md Moshiur Rahman of Hiroshima University, Japan called on vice-chancellor of the University of Dhaka Professor ABM Obaidul Islam at the DU on Wednesday......

item.title: 30 shops, houses, cattle burnt in fire in 3 dists
item.description: Several fire incidents burnt 30 shops, houses, cattle and valuables in Kurigram, Panchagarh and Noakhali on Tuesday night......

item.title: ‘July warriors’ lock Godagari press club over AL rehabilitation
item.description: A group of people identifying themselves as ‘July movement fighters’ locked the Godagari Upazila Press Club in Rajshahi on Tuesday, alleging that Awami League supporters were being rehabilitated there......Maps

item.title: Genocide Day observed
item.description: The nation on Wednesday observed the Genocide Day in remembrance of the brutality carried out by Pakistani Army on the unarmed Bangalees on the black night of March 25 in 1971 under the infamous ‘Operation Searchlight’......

item.title: BMP demands investigation, punishment for those involved
item.description: The Bangladesh Mahila Parishad in a statement on Wednesday demanded a fair and impartial investigation into the incident of acid attacks on a woman allegedly by her husband over dowry at Abhaynagar in Jashore......

item.title: ACC wants to show ex-Lt Gen Masud arrested in graft case, hearing on April 9
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission on Wednesday filed a petition with court to show retired Lieutenant General Masud Uddin Chowdhury arrested in a case over embezzling Tk 119 crore in the name of sending workers to Malaysia......

item.title: ICT chair’s Fb, WhatsApp accounts hacked
item.description: A general diary was filed on Wednesday with the Shahbagh police after the personal Facebook and WhatsApp accounts of International Crimes Tribunal-1 chairman Justice Golam Mortuza Mozumder had been hacked......

item.title: UGC directs universities to ensure free Wi-Fi facility
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh has decided to direct universities to take necessary initiatives to ensure free Wi-Fi facility in all public and private universities of the country as soon as possible......

item.title: Heat to cause 24 more deaths per lakh yearly in country: report
item.description: A report by the University of Chicago projects that rising temperatures could cause an additional 24 deaths per 1,00,000 people annually in Bangladesh......

item.title: Pry scholarship exams from April 15 in 61 dists
item.description: Primary scholarship examinations for 2025 are set to start from April 15 in 61 districts. In the three Chittagong Hill Tracts districts—Rangamati, Khagrachari and Bandarban—the exams will start from April 17 to allow the national minority communities to observe their year-end and New Year festivals......

item.title: 15pc journalists harassed sexually at workplaces: survey
item.description: Media personalities on Wednesday said that institutional weaknesses remain a major barrier to preventing and addressing sexual harassment in media houses......

item.title: Ukraine hits major Russian port in 400-drone barrage
item.description: Russia on Wednesday said a Ukrainian attack sparked a fire at a major port in the country’s northwest in a barrage of almost 400 drones, launched a day after a record Russian aerial assault on Ukraine......

item.title: 7 more errant healthcare facilities closed in Dhaka
item.description: The Directorate General of Health Services on Wednesday closed seven more private healthcare facilities in Dhaka over irregularities in providing services......

item.title: HSC exams to be held with common question papers
item.description: The education ministry on Wednesday has taken decision to hold Higher Secondary Certificate and equivalent examinations with common question papers this year......

item.title: Two killed as launch hits trawler in Patuakhali
item.description: Two people were killed when a three-storey passenger launch collided with a watermelon-laden trawler in the Lohalia River at Bauphal upazila of Patuakhali early Wednesday......

item.title: Govt to buy 2 cargos of LNG from spot market
item.description: Amid the Middle East conflicts, the government on Wednesday approved the urgent purchase of two more cargoes of liquefied natural gas from the spot market to address the ongoing energy crisis in the country......

item.title: Govt not to raise fuel price
item.description: The government on Wednesday dismissed any possibility of price hike of fuel oils while panic buying of the items amid short supply continued causing long queues at filling stations in the capital Dhaka and other cities in the country......Maps

item.title: Govt proposes Referendum Ordinance not be enacted
item.description: The government has proposed to the parliamentary special committee reviewing 133 ordinances issued during the interim government’s tenure that the Referendum Ordinance should not be enacted into law, arguing that it originated from a presidential order issued beyond legal jurisdiction......

item.title: Discrimination-free country still elusive
item.description: Fifty-five years after the independence of Bangladesh, the promise of a discrimination-free society remained unfulfilled as deep-rooted and widening inequalities keep shaping the country’s social, economic, and political landscape......

item.title: Nation recalls war heroes
item.description: The nation celebrates its 56th Independence Day today amid fervour and with due honour. Gun salutes, a military parade, discussions, cultural programmes, and other events would mark the celebrations of the historic day as the nation’s nine-month-long War of Independence......

item.title: Foreign debt soars by $10b in 2025
item.description: Bangladesh’s external debt rise sharply in 2025 as the government relied heavily on foreign borrowing to finance its budget and development spending......Bangladesh news updates

item.description: The Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday allowed Akij Food and Beverage Limited to raise Tk 500 crore through issuing zero-coupon bond......

item.title: Chattogram port handles 55,000 containers during Eid holidays
item.description: During the seven-day Eid-ul-Fitr holiday, Chattogram Port handled a total of 54,898 twenty-foot equivalent units or TEUs of containers, maintaining a steady pace of container handling......

item.title: B2B ties key to boost BIMSTEC trade: Pandey
item.description: BIMSTEC secretary general Indra Mani Pandey has underscored the importance of business-to-business or B2B relations to enhance intra-regional trade and investment across the Bay of Bengal region......

item.title: Eid holidays draw record crowds to Cox’s Bazar
item.description: Cox’s Bazar has turned into a bustling hub of festivity as an overwhelming influx of tourists floods the country’s premier seaside destination during the Eid-ul-Fitr holidays......

item.title: IEA members ‘ready’ to release more oil reserves
item.description: Head of the International Energy Agency said Wednesday that he was ‘ready’ to approve the release of more oil reserves if needed to cushion the impact of the Middle East war on global supplies......

item.title: Oil prices tumble, global stocks rally
item.description: Oil prices tumbled and stock markets rallied Wednesday on reports that the United States had sent a peace plan to Iran......

item.title: Vietnam lowers fuel prices after diesel doubled since Mideast war
item.description: Vietnam adjusted its fuel prices for a second time on Wednesday, bringing down costs after the price of diesel was more than doubled since the start of the war in the Middle East, trade ministry figures showed......

item.title: Data ‘canary’ shows economy already suffering from ME war
item.description: Soaring fuel prices due to the Middle East war and snarled supply chains are already hurting businesses, business survey data published Tuesday showed......

item.title: Power crunch pushes Sri Lanka to 4-day week
item.description: Millions of Sri Lankans enjoyed a government-ordered extra day off on Wednesday as the island nation battles an energy crisis triggered by the Middle East war......

item.title: New Mexico jury finds Meta liable for endangering children
item.description: A New Mexico jury on Tuesday found social media giant Meta liable for endangering children by making them vulnerable to predators on its platforms and other dangers......

item.title: Shipping giant Cosco resumes bookings to some Gulf countries
item.description: Chinese shipping giant Cosco said on Wednesday that it was resuming new bookings for container shipments to some Gulf countries, after a three-week suspension in response to the Middle East war......

item.title: Prime minister unveils commemorative stamp marking Independence Day
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday unveiled a commemorative postage stamp marking the Independence and National Day......
